message:
fix(rch): Refactor Codex CLI hook management for proper TOML parsing

Previously, hook detection and installation used naive string matching
(content.contains("[hooks]") && content.contains("rch")), which could
produce false positives or corrupt config files by appending duplicate
sections.

This change introduces robust TOML section handling:

- Add find_toml_section_range() to properly identify section boundaries
- Add is_pre_tool_use_line() and is_pre_tool_use_rch() for precise detection
- check_codex_cli_hook() now searches only within [hooks] section
- install_codex_cli_hook() finds or creates [hooks], updates pre_tool_use
  in place rather than blindly appending
- uninstall_codex_cli_hook() removes only the pre_tool_use = "rch" line
  instead of filtering all lines containing "rch" (which was dangerous)
- Add ensure_trailing_newline() for consistent file formatting

Benefits:
- No more duplicate [hooks] sections on repeated installs
- Uninstall won't accidentally remove unrelated lines containing "rch"
- Properly handles existing pre_tool_use with different values
- Idempotent: repeated install/uninstall operations are safe

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
